name: Enterprise Dark
tagline: Enterprise-grade dark technical style with charcoal foundations, restrained warm accents, and authoritative scale.

palette.description: Deep charcoal/navy background with white text; single warm orange accent used sparingly only in hero visuals and key highlights — never large-area; subtle purple-pink gradient graphics add depth in background.

typography.style: Modern geometric sans-serif. Extra-large bold headlines (48-80px equivalent) for immediate impact. No serifs. Single typeface family. Weight contrast handles hierarchy.

best_for: Enterprise AI, developer API platforms, B2B SaaS products that need to feel like serious infrastructure rather than consumer tools. Technical pitches, analyst briefings, capability showcases.
avoid_for: Consumer products, personal narratives, content that needs warmth or playfulness. Don't use if you need multi-color expressiveness or a light/friendly feel.
anti_patterns: using orange as a large-area background — it must remain a small accent to stay powerful
making the design feel light or consumer-friendly — this must maintain heavyweight seriousness
stacking too many decorative graphics — background graphics are supporting cast, not lead
warm color-adjacent palettes — this is a cold technical system with one warm point of contrast
